title: convolvulus marginatus
intro: Convolvulus marginatus, also known as the creeping morning glory, is a perennial vine native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as skin infections and wounds. The health benefits of Convolvulus marginatus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, with the roots and leaves typically being used for medicinal purposes.
